subject: english
learning_objectives: Define adverbial phrases and identify their types
Identify the four functions of prepositional phrases in sentences

sections.heading: Adverbial Phrases – Definition
sections.content: Adverbial phrases modify verbs, answering the questions: how? when? why? where? to what extent? They also modify adjectives, other adverbs, and prepositional phrases. Any group of words containing no finite verb and performing these functions is an adverbial phrase.

sections.heading: Types of Adverbial Phrases
sections.content:
Adverbial phrase of manner:
  He does the work with zeal / very well.

Adverbial phrase of time:
  I saw him in the morning.

Adverbial phrase of place:
  I met him at the supermarket.

Adverbial phrase of contrast:
  Despite her promise, she behaved differently.
  In spite of the good advice, she did not change her behaviour.
  His strength notwithstanding, he lost the fight.

sections.heading: Prepositional Phrases – Definition
sections.content:
A prepositional phrase begins with a preposition and ends with a noun phrase or pronoun.
Examples: to him | for the man | at the bank | about what he did

sections.heading: Functions of the Prepositional Phrase
sections.content:
(i) As an adverbial phrase:
  The police caught the thief in the garden.
  At a later date, we confronted him.

(ii) As an adjective:
  The man with a hat is our teacher.
  The news of his death shocked everyone.

(iii) As complement of a verb:
  We believe in what he says.

(iv) As complement of an adjective:
  We are sure of his arrival.
